headline: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From W. R. Berkley’s Q1 Earnings Call
summary: W. R. Berkley’s first quarter results reflected a mixed operating environment, with management citing heightened competition in certain insurance and reinsurance markets as a key factor. CEO W. Robert Berkley, Jr. described the industry as “more competitive today than it was yesterday,” pointing to increased activity from national carriers and evolving risk appetites. The quarter was further shaped by lower catastrophe losses, robust investment income, and resilience in core underwriting, despit

headline: Brown & Brown Q1 Earnings Top Estimates on Higher Commissions
summary: BRO Q1 earnings beat estimates as higher commissions, investment income and EBITDAC drive growth, despite flat organic revenues and rising expenses.
